title: Candidate Experience Blueprint and Manager Handoff
preview:
Overview:

text:
Overview:

This page defines the candidate-first hiring and onboarding workflow for Redwood Inference, and provides a standard manager handoff procedure designed to reduce time-to-productivity while preserving a consistent, high-quality candidate experience. The blueprint balances speed (time-to-offer), fairness (structured evaluation), and new-hire ramp (clear owner responsibilities).

Principles:
- Candidate-first: clear timelines, transparent feedback, and a single point of contact (recruiter) for each candidate.
- Structured decisions: use calibrated rubrics and scorecards for every role.
- Fast, predictable handoffs: manager acceptance and first-week readiness must meet SLAs below.
- Continuous improvement: capture candidate NPS and iterate quarterly.

Scope:
- Applies to all full-time and long-term contract technical and non-technical hires across Redwood Inference (hosted, dedi...

text:
Summary:
This playbook defines Redwood's Shadow Rotation & Talent Braiding program: a structured, time-boxed approach to accelerate new hire ramp, cross-team knowledge transfer, and internal mobility through short rotations, paired mentorship, and explicit success metrics. The program is intended for early-career hires, role-transfers, and high-potential talent preparing for expanded scope.

Goals and Success Metrics:
- Reduce median time-to-first-meaningful-PR by 25% within the first 90 days for participating engineers.
- Increase cross-team incident familiarity score by 30% for rotated staff (measured in post-rotation evals).
- Surface at least 10 internal mobility matches per quarter through the rotation pipeline.

Scope:
- Applies to: engineering, product, and platform ICs and managers entering a new domain or team at Redwood.
- Excludes: short-term contractors (<30 days) and indi...

title: New Engineer Autonomy and Success Blueprint
preview:
Summary
-------
This blueprint defines the end-to-end process to move a newly hired engineer from first-day orientation to independent contributor with measurable autonomy and impact. It covers the hiring handoff, staged onboarding milestones (30/60/90/180 days), manager and buddy responsibilities, performance and f...
text:
Summary
-------
This blueprint defines the end-to-end process to move a newly hired engineer from first-day orientation to independent contributor with measurable autonomy and impact. It covers the hiring handoff, staged onboarding milestones (30/60/90/180 days), manager and buddy responsibilities, performance and feedback cadence, role-readiness rubric, accommodations, and operational templates to standardize successful activation across teams.

Scope
-----
- Applies to all software engineering hires (IC levels L1–L6) across hosted, dedicated, and private product teams.
- Excludes contractor-only engagements where a separate contractor activation process exists (see related contractor playbook).
- Intended for hiring managers, people-ops partners, technical buddies, and platform/infra owners who contribute to onboarding.

Objectives and success criteria

text:
Overview
======

Purpose: This document defines Redwood's Probation Acceleration Program (PAP) and the structured 360° development feedback cycle used during the first 120 days of employment. The goal is to support new hires (ICs and new managers) with rapid clarity on expectations, early capability development, and fair, calibrated assessment at the end of probation.

Scope
-----
- Applies to all full-time and fixed-term hires (individual contributors and managers) in global teams, excluding contractors on < 90-day engagements.
- Covers the first 120 calendar days from start date: 30/60/90 checkpoints and an optional 120-day stretch review for high-impact roles.

Principles
----------
- Transparent expectations: Every hire receives a role-specific success plan before Day 1.
- Frequent, documented touchpoints: Weekly 1:1s plus formal checkpoint notes at 30/60/90 days.
- Development-fi...

title: Leader Feedback and Performance Sprint Guide
preview:
Purpose
-------
This guide defines a lightweight, repeatable approach for managers and people-ops to run short, outcome-oriented performance cycles called "performance sprints". The goal is to increase feedback frequency, reduce ambiguity in expectations, and create actionable development plans aligned to team prior...
text:
Purpose
-------
This guide defines a lightweight, repeatable approach for managers and people-ops to run short, outcome-oriented performance cycles called "performance sprints". The goal is to increase feedback frequency, reduce ambiguity in expectations, and create actionable development plans aligned to team priorities.

Scope
-----
Applies to all managers and ICs at Redwood Inference. The approach is intended to complement annual performance reviews, not replace them. Use performance sprints for: onboarding checkpoints, quarterly goal pulses, skill development pushes, and short-term performance recovery.

Principles
----------
- Frequent: shorter cycles (4–8 weeks) make adjustments predictable and lower-stakes.
- Transparent: shared goals, measurable outcomes, and documented follow-ups.
- Coaching-first: emphasize growth; corrective steps should escalate only when coaching fails.
-...

text:
Summary

Purpose: This playbook defines a repeatable end-to-end flow from hiring decision to 90-day impact. It blends hiring committee practice, preboarding, a structured 30/60/90 plan, manager coaching checkpoints, and objective ramp metrics so new hires hit predictable outcomes. Use this as the canonical manager + people-ops coordination checklist for IC and manager hires.

Audience
- Hiring managers
- People Ops / Talent partners
- Interviewers and hiring committee members
- New hire onboarding sponsors and buddies

Key outcomes
- Time-to-autonomy target: role-dependent (see Ramp SLOs table)
- New-hire satisfaction (first 30 days) >= 85%
- Hiring decision consistency across committees via a shared rubric
- Manager-led coaching cadence established in first 14 days

1) Hiring and Decisioning: Structured Committee Workflow

Before finalizing an offer:
